mnemonics.title: SPIN to find Stationary Distribution
mnemonics.content: S - Set up pi * P = pi equations; P - Pick one equation to replace (they are dependent); I - Insert the normalization condition sum(pi_i) = 1; N - Now solve the system. Remember: always SPIN through these four steps when finding a stationary distribution.

mnemonics.title: Absorbing State = Hotel California
mnemonics.content: Think of an absorbing state like the Hotel California: 'You can check in any time you like, but you can never leave.' If p_ii = 1, the state traps you forever. Look for rows in the transition matrix with a 1 on the diagonal and 0s everywhere else -- those are your Hotel California states.

mnemonics.title: CANE for Absorbing Chain Analysis
mnemonics.content: C - Canonical form: rearrange P into [[I, O], [R, Q]]; A - Assemble (I - Q); N - compute N = (I - Q)^(-1) (the fundamental matrix); E - Extract results: row sums of N give expected steps, B = N*R gives absorption probabilities. Use the CANE to walk through every absorbing chain problem.
